span.p: Cost allocation tags on + Cost Explorer readable?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Turn on cost allocation tags + CUR/Cost Explorer FIRST
you cannot optimize what you cannot attribute

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Decompose the spend by usage type — what is the biggest driver?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Eliminate waste first
delete/stop — free, reversible, no commitment

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Network cost — VPC endpoints, same-AZ, CloudFront
see the NAT-gateway scenario

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Rightsize to observed utilization
Compute Optimizer / CloudWatch p95

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: S3 lifecycle / Intelligent-Tiering, EBS gp2->gp3

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Footprint now stable + predictable for 1yr+?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Commit the BASELINE only
Compute Savings Plans / RIs on the floor, not the peak

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Stay on-demand / Spot for fault-tolerant
revisit commitment after rightsizing settles

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Fault-tolerant / interruptible workload on top?

span.p: Spot for the interruptible layer above the committed baseline
